perturbation.explanation: The term 'Contractor' is replaced with 'Consultant'. Later on in the document the term Contractor is mentioned, leading to an in-text contradition of whom is being reffered. This creates uncertainty about the roles and responsibilities defined throughout the agreement. This change can cause in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 In this Agreement, the following definitions shall apply: Agreement means this Non -Disclosure Agreement for Project Proposals. Confidential Information means all information relating to the Project o r the Contractor’s proposal for the Project and all information relating to the nature of either Party’s business, affairs or trade secrets, which either Party directly or indirectly receives or acquires from the other Party, or the other Party’s represent ative, either in writing or verbally (but in the case of verbal information, only that which is reduced to writing by the Disclosing Party and delivered to the Receiving Party within 10 days of the verbal disclosure) , or through observation of the Project, except information falling into any one or more of the following categories: (a) information which the Receiving Party can show was in its possession on a non - confidential basis before receipt of the information from the Disclosing Party; (b) information which is lawfully in the public domain at the time of the Receiving Party’s receipt of the information from the Disclosing Party, but not including the Company’s request for proposals for the Project; (c) information which, after the Receiving Party’s receipt of the i nformation from the Disclosing Party, becomes part of the public domain through no act of the Receiving Party or of any third party under an obligation of confidence with respect to such information, but only after such information becomes part of the publ ic domain; or (d) information which, after receipt of the information by the Receiving Party, is lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party, but only after such information is so received, and provided such third party is under no obligation o f confidence with respect to such information. Disclosing Party means the Party disclosing Confidential Information. Effective Date means the date on which this Agreement is deemed to be effective, as first written above. Page 2 of 6 Initial Term means the 1 year period commencing on the Effective Date and expiring at 11:59 pm. on _____________ , 20__. Party means either the Company or Contractor and Parties means both of them. Permitted Purpose means the purpose for which the Confidential Information is disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party and which is more particularly described as: (i) responding to the Company’s request for proposals for the Project, in the case of the Contrac tor; (ii) assessing the Contractor’s proposal, in the case of the Company; and (iii) if applicable, negotiation by the Parties of a definitive agreement related to the Project. Permitted Recipients means those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Inform ation for the Permitted Purpose and who are officers, directors, employees, agents or contractors (at any level) of the Receiving Party or its affiliates, or anyone else for whom the Receiving Party takes responsibility for under this Agreement. Project means the Company’s project in relation to which it is seeking proposals for performance of work or services or the provision of materials or equipment. Receiving Party means the Party receiving Confidential Information from the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a party as 'Contractor'. The changed text replaces this with 'Consultant'. The contradicted text uses the term 'Contractor' and defines obligations and meanings based on it. By changing 'Contractor' to 'Consultant', the contract now refers to two potentially different entities without clear distinction, creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition which states that in-text contradictions arise when a contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: Party means either the Company or Contractor and Parties means both of them.
perturbation.changed_text: Party means either the Company or Vendor and Parties means both of them.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Contractor' is replaced with 'Vendor' in the definition of 'Party'. This is a direct contradiction because earlier, in the preamble, the second party was defined as 'Contractor' (now 'Consultant') but in the definitions section, 'Party' is now either the 'Company' or 'Vendor'. Now within the document, which party is the contractor and which is the vendor. This change can cause in-text contradiction.

Confidential Information means all information relating to the Project o r the Contractor’s proposal for the Project and all information relating to the nature of either Party’s business, affairs or trade secrets, which either Party directly or indirectly receives or acquires from the other Party, or the other Party’s represent ative, either in writing or verbally (but in the case of verbal information, only that which is reduced to writing by the Disclosing Party and delivered to the Receiving Party within 10 days of the verbal disclosure) , or through observation of the Project, except information falling into any one or more of the following categories: (a) information which the Receiving Party can show was in its possession on a non - confidential basis before receipt of the information from the Disclosing Party; (b) information which is lawfully in the public domain at the time of the Receiving Party’s receipt of the information from the Disclosing Party, but not including the Company’s request for proposals for the Project; (c) information which, after the Receiving Party’s receipt of the i nformation from the Disclosing Party, becomes part of the public domain through no act of the Receiving Party or of any third party under an obligation of confidence with respect to such information, but only after such information becomes part of the publ ic domain; or (d) information which, after receipt of the information by the Receiving Party, is lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party, but only after such information is so received, and provided such third party is under no obligation o f confidence with respect to such information. Permitted Recipients means those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Inform ation for the Permitted Purpose and who are officers, directors, employees, agents or contractors (at any level) of the Receiving Party or its affiliates, or anyone else for whom the Receiving Party takes responsibility for under this Agreement. Project means the Company’s project in relation to which it is seeking proposals for performance of work or services or the provision of materials or equipment. Receiving Party means the Party receiving Confidential Information from the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Contractor' to 'Vendor' in the definition of 'Party' creates a direct contradiction. The original text defines 'Party' as either 'Company' or 'Contractor'. The changed text redefines 'Party' as either 'Company' or 'Vendor'. The contradicted text uses the term 'Contractor' in the context of the 'Permitted Purpose' section, specifically referring to the party responding to the company's proposal. By replacing 'Contractor' with 'Vendor' in the definition of 'Party', the document now uses these two terms interchangeably without clear definition, directly contradicting the meaning of the initial agreement. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty about which party is the 'Contractor' and which is the 'Vendor,' thereby causing an in-text contradiction that falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Project means the Company’s project in relation to which it is seeking proposals for performance of work or services or the provision of materials or equipment.
perturbation.changed_text: Project means the Company’s venture in relation to which it is seeking bids for construction or the arrangement of physical resources.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'project' to 'venture', 'proposals' to 'bids', 'performance of work or services or the provision of materials or equipment' to 'construction or the arrangement of physical resources' creates ambiguity. Now there is uncertainty on what kind of Project is the original text reffering to. The new definition is now creating a scope limitation.

Confidential Information means all information relating to the Project o r the Contractor’s proposal for the Project and all information relating to the nature of either Party’s business, affairs or trade secrets, which either Party directly or indirectly receives or acquires from the other Party, or the other Party’s represent ative, either in writing or verbally (but in the case of verbal information, only that which is reduced to writing by the Disclosing Party and delivered to the Receiving Party within 10 days of the verbal disclosure) , or through observation of the Project, except information falling into any one or more of the following categories: (a) information which the Receiving Party can show was in its possession on a non - confidential basis before receipt of the information from the Disclosing Party; (b) information which is lawfully in the public domain at the time of the Receiving Party’s receipt of the information from the Disclosing Party, but not including the Company’s request for proposals for the Project; (c) information which, after the Receiving Party’s receipt of the i nformation from the Disclosing Party, becomes part of the public domain through no act of the Receiving Party or of any third party under an obligation of confidence with respect to such information, but only after such information becomes part of the publ ic domain; or (d) information which, after receipt of the information by the Receiving Party, is lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party, but only after such information is so received, and provided such third party is under no obligation o f confidence with respect to such information. Permitted Recipients means those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Inform ation for the Permitted Purpose and who are officers, directors, employees, agents or contractors (at any level) of the Receiving Party or its affiliates, or anyone else for whom the Receiving Party takes responsibility for under this Agreement. Project means the Company’s venture in relation to which it is seeking bids for construction or the arrangement of physical resources. perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the definition of 'Project' from a general term encompassing 'performance of work or services or the provision of materials or equipment' to a more specific term focused on 'construction or the arrangement of physical resources'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is actually in the changed text) because the contradicted text defines 'Project' using the new, narrower definition, while other parts of the document, particularly 1.1, refer to 'Project Proposals,' which would now be ambiguously limited to only construction-related projects, thus creating a scope limitation and an in-text contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Permitted Purpose means the purpose for which the Confidential Information is disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party and which is more particularly described as: (i) responding to the Company’s request for proposals for the Project, in the case of the Contrac tor; (ii) assessing the Contractor’s proposal, in the case of the Company; and (iii) if applicable, negotiation by the Parties of a definitive agreement related to the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Permitted Objective means the reason why the Confidential Information is provided by the Disclosing Side to the Receiving Side and which is specifically identified as: (i) replying to the Company’s solicitation for bids for the Venture, if the Advisor is the Receiving Side; (ii) analyzing the Advisor’s submission, if the Company is the Receiving Side; and (iii) if relevant, conversation by the Sides of a conclusive arrangement connected to the Venture.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Permitted Purpose' to 'Permitted Objective', 'Confidential Information is disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party' to 'Confidential Information is provided by the Disclosing Side to the Receiving Side', 'responding to the Company’s request for proposals for the Project, in the case of the Contrac tor' to 'replying to the Company’s solicitation for bids for the Venture, if the Advisor is the Receiving Side' and other terms here makes this text a lot more contradictory to other terms in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Company and the Contractor believe it is in their mutual best interest to disclose information to support the Permitted Purpose. Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other for the Permitted Purpose, which disclosure will be governed by the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Permitted Purpose' to 'Permitted Objective', 'Disclosing Party' to 'Disclosing Side', 'Contractor' to 'Advisor', and 'Project' to 'Venture' introduces misaligned terminology. The contradicted text refers to 'Permitted Purpose', 'Company', and 'Contractor'. The changed text uses entirely different terms for the same concepts. This creates a direct conflict, as the contradicted text relies on terms no longer consistently defined or used, leading to confusion and uncertainty about the scope of the agreement and making enforcement unclear because the definitions do not line up, adhering to the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Permitted Recipients means those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Inform ation for the Permitted Purpose and who are officers, directors, employees, agents or contractors (at any level) of the Receiving Party or its affiliates, or anyone else for whom the Receiving Party takes responsibility for under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Authorized Personnel means individuals who require access to Private Details for the Permitted Objective and who are executive managers, administrators, workforce, representatives, or consultants (at any echelon) of the Receiving Side or its connected bodies, or any other party for whom the Receiving Side accepts liability under this Compact.
perturbation.explanation: Here we are changing all of the text, again making the meaning of the text more contradictory to the original definitions as previously stated. Changing 'Permitted Recipients' to 'Authorized Personnel', 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details', 'Permitted Purpose' to 'Permitted Objective', 'officers, directors, employees, agents or contractors (at any level) of the Receiving Party or its affiliates' to 'executive managers, administrators, workforce, representatives, or consultants (at any echelon) of the Receiving Side or its connected bodies' and 'under this Agreement' to 'under this Compact' and etc
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 Before disclosing any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to the Permitted Recipients, the Receiving Party shall identify to the Permitted Recipients that it is the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and is subject to disclosure and usage restrictions, and shall ensure that the Permitted Recipients have: (a) execut ed Appendix A – Permitted Recipient’s Acknowledgement; or Page 3 of 6 (b) are bound in writing by confidentiality terms, policies or procedures no less restrictive than those in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Permitted Recipients' to 'Authorized Personnel', 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details' and 'under this Agreement' to 'under this Compact' creates a contradiction. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Permitted Recipients' and 'Confidential Information'. Since the new text redefines these terms to 'Authorized Personnel' and 'Private Details' and uses 'Compact' instead of 'Agreement', the receiving party cannot ensure that these 'Authorized Personnel' execute 'Appendix A – Permitted Recipient’s Acknowledgement' which is referring to the OLD terms. The change directly alters the terms used in the contradicted text, leading to a contradiction. The Misaligned Terminology perturbation directly causes a strong in-text contradiction.
